FooterHelp.title: How To Use TrustLedger
FooterHelp.intro: Use this guide when you need the fastest path through contract creation, dashboard navigation, and dispute resolution.
FooterHelp.stepCreateBody: Open New Contract, choose whether you are the client or freelancer, add the counterparty wallet, amount, timeline, and document link, then send the offer.
FooterHelp.stepDashboardBody: Use Dashboard to fund accepted offers, submit work, approve delivery, open disputes, claim deadlines, and rate the other party.
FooterHelp.stepJurorBody: Disputed contracts link into arbitration. Jurors use the Juror page to register stake and the dispute page to commit and reveal votes.
FooterHelp.stepCreateTaskOne: Choose client or freelancer before drafting.
FooterHelp.stepCreateTaskTwo: Add wallet, email, payment, timeline, and document details.
FooterHelp.stepCreateTaskThree: Review the proposal before your wallet signs.
FooterHelp.stepDashboardTaskOne: Check funding, delivery, approval, and dispute state.
FooterHelp.stepDashboardTaskTwo: Open or decrypt the contract document when needed.
FooterHelp.stepDashboardTaskThree: Use only the action buttons available to your wallet.
FooterHelp.stepDraftBody: Use encrypted share links or live rooms when both parties need to review contract language before deployment.
FooterHelp.stepDraftTaskOne: Create one encrypted link and send the session key separately.
FooterHelp.stepDraftTaskTwo: Use live sync only when both wallets are ready to co-edit.
FooterHelp.stepDraftTaskThree: Do not reuse older links after a newer link is generated.
FooterHelp.stepDocumentBody: Attach an encrypted PDF or provide a trusted document URI so the on-chain proposal can hash the right agreement.
FooterHelp.stepDocumentTaskOne: Keep written terms and uploaded files aligned.
FooterHelp.stepDocumentTaskTwo: Use encryption before uploading sensitive documents.
FooterHelp.stepDocumentTaskThree: Preview the URI before confirming the wallet transaction.
FooterHelp.stepJurorTaskOne: Register only with stake you are prepared to lock.
FooterHelp.stepJurorTaskTwo: Commit votes first, then reveal with the saved salt.
FooterHelp.stepJurorTaskThree: Claim rewards only after the dispute is finalized.
FooterHelp.stepArbitrationBody: Dispute pages keep evidence, juror votes, rulings, and payout execution connected to the original contract.
FooterHelp.stepArbitrationTaskOne: Submit clear evidence and requested completion percentage.
FooterHelp.stepArbitrationTaskThree: Review final payouts before executing or claiming rewards.
FooterHelp.stepReputationBody: Use reputation history to inspect completed work ratings and recovery progress for a wallet.
FooterHelp.stepReputationTaskTwo: Check average score, rating count, and recovery state.
FooterHelp.stepReputationTaskThree: Submit ratings only after a contract is finished.
FooterHelp.stepWalletBody: Wallet access stays in the wallet provider. TrustLedger only remembers a safe connector label and disconnects after inactivity.
FooterHelp.stepWalletTaskOne: Reconnect after force refreshes, deployments, or session expiry.
FooterHelp.stepWalletTaskTwo: Never share private keys, seed phrases, or admin tokens.
FooterHelp.stepWalletTaskThree: Expect automatic local disconnect after inactivity.
FooterHelp.stepLegalBody: The legal center indexes policy, risk, privacy, compliance, and security references for review.
FooterHelp.stepLegalTaskOne: Read the source document before relying on a translated summary.
FooterHelp.stepLegalTaskTwo: Check security reporting guidance before disclosing issues.
FooterHelp.stepLegalTaskThree: Ask for review before changing legal obligations.
FooterHelp.stepFaqBody: The FAQ explains wallet setup, funding, contract states, disputes, and testnet faucet usage.
FooterHelp.stepFaqTaskOne: Read the relevant question before moving funds.
FooterHelp.stepFaqTaskTwo: Use GitHub Discussions for open-ended support.
FooterHelp.stepFaqTaskThree: Check deployment and faucet docs for testnet issues.
FooterHelp.stepAnalyticsBody: Analytics shows public contract counts, status trends, reputation summaries, and safe local preferences for the connected wallet.
FooterHelp.stepAnalyticsTaskOne: Connect the wallet you want to inspect.
FooterHelp.stepAnalyticsTaskTwo: Review only public on-chain metrics and safe local app preferences.
FooterHelp.stepAnalyticsTaskThree: Use the dashboard and reputation links for deeper follow-up.

Analytics.title: Privacy-Safe Wallet Analytics
Analytics.intro: See how your connected wallet interacts with TrustLedger using public contract state, reputation summaries, and local app preferences. TrustLedger does not scrape private wallet data, document contents, emails, session keys, private keys, or seed phrases.
Analytics.connectBody: Analytics is wallet-scoped. Connect the wallet you want to inspect, then TrustLedger will summarize public on-chain contract state and safe local preferences from this browser.
Analytics.privacyBody: This page reads public TrustLedger contract state and safe local app settings only. It does not read private wallet material, encrypted draft bodies, uploaded documents, emails, or hidden browser data.
Analytics.privacyScoreDetail: Derived from dispute rate and hold-back settings. Higher is cleaner.
Analytics.statusBody: Status bars summarize public TrustLedger contracts that include this wallet.
Analytics.emptyInsight: No public contracts were found for this wallet yet.
Analytics.actionsBody: Use these metrics to decide whether to inspect contract details, review reputation history, or create a cleaner contract workflow.
Analytics.roleMixBody: How this wallet appears across visible contracts.
Analytics.outcomeSnapshotBody: Visible completion, active work, and disputes for this wallet.
Analytics.activityBody: A lightweight view of how this wallet’s visible contracts are distributed.
Analytics.lineChartAria: Line chart of wallet contract states
Analytics.insightNoContracts: No public TrustLedger contracts were found for this connected wallet yet.
Analytics.insightHighDispute: A higher dispute rate is visible in public contract states. Review terms, milestones, and evidence quality before new work starts.
Analytics.insightHealthy: Most visible contracts are completed or resolved, which indicates a healthy TrustLedger workflow history.
Analytics.insightDeveloping: Your public activity is still developing. Track funded, submitted, approved, and disputed states as you use the platform.

Home.subtext: TrustLedger holds your ETH in a smart contract escrow. Funds release on client approval - or via decentralized juror arbitration if there's a dispute.
Home.featureEscrowDesc: ETH is locked in the contract. Neither party can move it unilaterally.
Home.featureArbitrationDesc: Staked jurors vote on outcomes via a commit-reveal system. Minority voters are slashed.
Home.featureWarrantyDesc: Optionally retain 5-15% of payment until a warranty period elapses.
Home.evmEscrowBody: Use the current Ethereum Sepolia escrow flow for real contract proposals and testing.
Home.solanaPreviewBody: Use Solana Devnet preview mode for SOL-denominated drafts and native-program metadata before submitting through the Solana flow.

loading.title: Loading TrustLedger
loading.subtitle: We’re preparing your secure escrow workspace.

Dashboard.connectWallet: Connect your wallet to see your contracts.
Dashboard.title: Dashboard
Dashboard.emptyDescription: TrustLedger contracts appear here after both wallets agree on terms and escrow is funded or ready to fund.
Dashboard.emptyStepCreate: Create a contract with the other party's wallet address, payment amount, deadline, and document link.
Dashboard.emptyStepFund: The client funds escrow after the offer is accepted, so neither party controls the money alone.
Dashboard.emptyStepReview: Track delivery, approve work, or open a dispute from the contract card.
Dashboard.walkthroughIntro: TrustLedger holds freelance payment in escrow until work is approved, or a dispute is resolved by jurors.
Dashboard.walkthroughStepCreateBody: Add the counterparty wallet, amount, timeline, and a contract document link. The other party reviews before work starts.
Dashboard.walkthroughStepTrackBody: Dashboard cards show funding, delivery, approval, deadlines, documents, and any action your wallet can take.
Dashboard.walkthroughStepResolveBody: If work is disputed, the card links to arbitration so evidence, votes, and payouts stay tied to the same contract.
Dashboard.workSubmitted: Work submitted — waiting for client approval.
Dashboard.countdownProjectDetail: Proof of work is due by
Dashboard.countdownAcceptanceDetail: Approval or dispute window expires at
Dashboard.walkthroughStepCreateTaskRole: Choose client or freelancer before drafting.
Dashboard.walkthroughStepCreateTaskDetails: Add counterparty wallet, amount, timeline, and document source.
Dashboard.walkthroughStepCreateTaskPreview: Preview the proposal before your wallet signs.
Dashboard.walkthroughStepTrackTaskState: Confirm funding, delivery, and approval state.
Dashboard.walkthroughStepTrackTaskDocument: Open the document or decrypt it with the shared passphrase.
Dashboard.walkthroughStepTrackTaskActions: Use only the action buttons available to your connected wallet.
Dashboard.walkthroughStepResolveTaskEvidence: Submit evidence with a clear requested completion percentage.
Dashboard.walkthroughStepResolveTaskJurors: Jurors commit votes first, then reveal with the saved salt.
Dashboard.walkthroughStepResolveTaskPayouts: Review final payouts and claim available rewards.
Dashboard.deliverableUrlPlaceholder: https://… or ipfs://… or Qm… / baf…

Create.connectWallet: Connect your wallet to propose a contract.
Create.subtitleClient: You propose the terms. The freelancer reviews and accepts, then you fund the escrow to start the project.
Create.subtitleFreelancer: You propose the terms; the client reviews and locks the {token} in escrow on acceptance. Funds are released once you deliver and the client approves — or a dispute is resolved.
Create.bewareWarning: BEWARE: The proposed currency is locked on submit AND freelancer agreement!
Create.usdcNotSupported: USDC not supported on this network.
Create.solDraftNote: SOL draft mode is active for Solana Devnet. Use it to prepare and share SOL-denominated terms; final custody submits through the Solana native-program flow.
Create.freelancerAddressHint: The wallet that will review, accept, and deliver the work.
Create.clientAddressHint: The wallet that will review, fund, and approve this contract.
Create.freelancerEmailHint: A signed magic link will be sent here so the freelancer can review and accept via the web.
Create.clientEmailHint: A signed magic link will be sent here so the client can review and accept via the web.
Create.escrowHintClientUsdc: Total USDC you agree to lock in escrow once the freelancer accepts. You will approve the USDC transfer when funding.
Create.escrowHintClientEth: Total ETH you agree to lock in escrow once the freelancer accepts.
Create.escrowHintFreelancerUsdc: Total USDC the client will lock in escrow on acceptance. The client must approve the USDC transfer before funding.
Create.escrowHintFreelancerEth: Total ETH the client will lock in escrow on acceptance.
Create.escrowHintSol: Total SOL for this Solana Devnet draft. Share and review these terms here, then submit final custody through the Solana native-program flow.
Create.contractDocumentUriHint: IPFS link or URL to the off-chain agreement. A hash of this is stored on-chain.
Create.clickToBrowse: Click to browse or drop a file
Create.passphraseHint: Share this with the other party via a separate secure channel. You cannot decrypt without it.
Create.pinataJwtHint: Required to upload the document to IPFS.
Create.arweaveBackupTitle: Permanent backup to Arweave (optional - for long-term legal retention)
Create.arweaveUploadFailed: Arweave upload failed - check wallet balance.
Create.estimatedDurationHint: How long the project should take.
Create.bufferFactorHint: Project deadline = duration × buffer / 1000. E.g. 1200 = 1.2× buffer.
Create.acceptanceWindowHint: How long you have to review submitted work. Minimum 2 days.
Create.arbitrationFeeHint: Percentage of escrow set aside for jurors if a dispute is opened (0-50%).
Create.warrantyHoldbackHint: Portion withheld from the freelancer until the warranty period elapses.
Create.warrantyPeriodHint: How long the hold-back is locked after work is approved.
Create.usdcApprovalNote: You will need to approve the escrow contract to spend your USDC before funding.
Create.freelancerWillReview: The freelancer will review and accept. You will then be prompted to fund the escrow to start the project.
Create.failedToSendMagicLink: Failed to send magic link - check server env vars.
Create.previewSubtitle: This preview updates as you edit. Nothing is deployed until you review and confirm.
Create.draftSavedNote: Draft terms are saved locally in this browser so you can keep editing before deployment.
Create.confirmSendQuestion: Are you sure you want to send this to the {role}?
Create.confirmSendDescription: After you confirm, your wallet will open and the contract proposal will be submitted on-chain. Keep editing if any term is wrong.
Create.draftCacheWarning: Do Not Clear Cookies Or Browser Cache Until You Finish, Because That Resets Local Draft Progress.
Create.secureDraftBody: Write terms in Markdown, HTML, or plain text before deployment. Shared drafts are encrypted in the browser and require an allowed wallet plus a separate session key to decrypt. Start a live room only when both parties want ongoing co-editing.
Create.termsPlaceholder: Write the contract terms before deployment.
Create.enterCounterpartyWallet: Enter The Counterparty Wallet In Contract Details Below
Create.replaceLinkWarning: Creating another link replaces the active link and key shown here. Do not use older links or keys after generating a newer one; encrypted snapshot URLs that were already shared cannot be revoked without a backend revocation service.
Create.liveRoomStatus: Live Room {roomId}: edits are encrypted before leaving the browser. Last remote update: {time}.
Create.liveRoomLoadAdvice: Unable to load the live draft room. Check the session link, session key, wallet, or room expiration.
Create.encryptedDraftNotice: This URL contains an encrypted draft for {wallets}. Connect the matching wallet, paste the separate key, then import.
Create.contractDocumentDraftHint: Upload an encrypted contract PDF or enter an existing URI after drafting. If you also wrote terms above, keep both copies aligned before preview and submission; the uploaded or manual URI is the document source hashed for the on-chain proposal.
Create.solanaSimulationReady: TrustLedger will simulate the Solana escrow instruction before requesting your signature.
Create.solanaProgramMissing: Set NEXT_PUBLIC_SOLANA_PROGRAM_ID before SOL custody can be submitted.
Create.connectEvmWalletHint: Use the header wallet button before submitting ETH or USDC escrow.

Juror.connectWallet: Connect your wallet to manage your juror registration.
Juror.title: Juror Panel
Juror.subtitle: Stake ETH to become eligible for dispute committees. Earn fees for majority votes.
Juror.stakeLockedFor7Days: Stake is locked for 7 days after deposit. Eligible after {date}.
Juror.reputationBelowMin: Reputation below minimum (20). Minority votes reduce reputation by 10 each.
Juror.registerSubtitle: Stake at least 0.01 ETH. Stake is locked 7 days before you become eligible. Minority votes slash 10% of your stake.
Juror.registered: Registered. Stake is locked for {days} days before eligibility activates.
Juror.assignedDisputesSubtitle: Recent selected juror cases from the arbitration contract.
Juror.noAssignedDisputes: No assigned disputes found in the recent on-chain window.
Juror.localDraftHistorySubtitle: Unsubmitted evidence drafts saved in this browser for juror and party review.
Juror.noLocalDrafts: No local arbitration drafts found on this device.
Juror.assetConfiguredSoon: On-chain {asset} staking is configured for this network and unlocks shortly.
Juror.assetUnavailable: {asset} staking is not yet available on this network.
Juror.usdcStakeSubtitle: Deposit USDC into the staking vault to earn rewards.

Reputation.title: Reputation
Reputation.subtitle: Look up cumulative escrow ratings from completed TrustLedger contracts.
Reputation.connectToLookUp: Connect to look up your own address quickly.
Reputation.recoveryProgressValue: {progress} / 3 toward clearing {pending} low {pending, plural, one {rating} other {ratings}}
Reputation.scoresNote: Scores are submitted by contract parties after completion (1-100). Dispute resolutions may apply automatic penalties. Receiving 3 scores ≥ 70 after a low score (≤ 30) earns a +50 recovery bonus.
Reputation.failedToRead: Failed to read reputation. Check that you are on the correct network.
Reputation.registryNotConfigured: Reputation is not available for this TrustLedger deployment yet.
Reputation.trustLedgerNotConfiguredWithHint: TrustLedger is not configured for {network}. Switch your wallet to {networks}, or set this network's NEXT_PUBLIC_* contract address variables and redeploy.
Reputation.trustLedgerNotConfiguredNoHint: TrustLedger is not configured for {network}. Set the NEXT_PUBLIC_* contract address variables for this network and redeploy.
Reputation.registryLookupError: Could not discover the reputation registry for this network. Check that your wallet is connected to a supported TrustLedger network.

Arbitration.youAreSelected: You are selected - commit your vote
Arbitration.bondRequired: Bond required: <amountStrong>{amount} ETH</amountStrong> (1.5× fee pool). Returned if you win; forfeited if you lose.
Arbitration.appealWindowElapsed: Appeal window elapsed - ruling can be executed.
Arbitration.notPartyOrJuror: You are not a party or juror in this dispute.
Arbitration.commitInstructions: Choose a completion percentage (0 = fully refund client, 100 = fully pay freelancer). A random salt will be generated and saved locally - do not clear your browser storage before revealing.
Arbitration.saltSaved: Your salt and completion % have been saved to localStorage. You will need them to reveal.
Arbitration.revealInstructions: Enter the same percentage and salt you committed with. These are pre-filled from localStorage if available.
Arbitration.saltInvalid: Salt must be a 0x-prefixed 32-byte hex string (66 characters).
Arbitration.noEvidence: No evidence has been submitted yet.

Client.reviewTerms: Review the terms and document below. Accepting locks {amount} in escrow.
Client.usdcNote: This contract uses USDC. You will approve the transfer in a separate step before funding.
Client.notAwaiting: Contract is not awaiting acceptance. It may already be accepted, rejected, or withdrawn.
Client.escrowFunded: The escrow is funded and the project deadline timer has started.
Client.proposalDeclined: The proposal has been declined. No funds were transferred.
Client.wrongWalletDesc: This link is for {address}. Please switch to that account.
Client.connectWalletPrompt: Connect the wallet at {address} to review contract #{id}.

Freelancer.clientProposes: The client proposes {amount} in escrow. Review the offer below. Accepting signals your agreement — the client will then fund the escrow to start the project deadline.
Freelancer.clientWillFund: The client will now be notified to fund the escrow. The project deadline starts once funds are locked.
Freelancer.offerHasBeenDeclined: The offer has been declined. No funds were held.
Freelancer.contractNotAwaiting: This contract is not awaiting your acceptance (current: {status}). It may already be accepted, rejected, or withdrawn.
Freelancer.wrongWalletDesc: This link is for {address}. Please switch to that account.
Freelancer.connectWalletPrompt: Connect the wallet at {address} to review contract #{id}.

Common.connectedAs: Connected as {address}. Click to open wallet options.
Common.analyticsHint: View wallet-safe stats and public contract metrics.
Common.walletMenuHint: Open wallet-safe account tools, analytics, public status, and project background.
Common.walletRequiredBody: Connect your wallet to continue. TrustLedger only shows wallet-scoped workspace data after your address is available in this browser.
Common.walletRequiredHint: Use the same wallet you use for contracts, juror actions, reputation, and account preferences.
Common.walletRequiredChecklistAddress: Confirm the wallet address before signing.
Common.walletRequiredChecklistSecrets: Never enter seed phrases or private keys.
Common.walletRequiredChecklistNetwork: Switch networks in your wallet when prompted.

Decrypt.title: Decrypt AES-256-GCM document
Decrypt.decryptionFailed: Decryption failed — wrong passphrase or corrupted bundle.

FAQ.title: Read this before you move money or if you have general questions.
FAQ.introBefore: Use this page when you are creating an escrow, debugging a wallet issue, or deciding what to do after a contract changes state. Start a discussion in

one.question: Do I need crypto experience to use TrustLedger?
one.answer: No. The app guides you through wallet connection, escrow funding, work submission, approval, and disputes with plain-language labels.

two.question: What happens to funds after a contract is created?
two.answer: Funds stay in the smart contract escrow. They release only after client approval, acceptance-window expiry, deadline recovery, or an arbitration ruling.

three.question: Why do I need Sepolia ETH?
three.answer: Sepolia ETH pays gas for development and testnet transactions. It has no real value, but faucets rate-limit it to prevent abuse.

four.question: Can I pay with USDC instead of ETH?
four.answer: Yes, supported ERC-20 escrows use token approval and funding instead of sending native ETH directly with the acceptance transaction.

five.question: What should I do when a transaction fails?
five.answer: Check that the connected wallet is the expected client, freelancer, or juror wallet; confirm the network; then read the simulation or wallet error before retrying.

six.question: How are disputes resolved?
six.answer: Staked jurors commit hidden votes, reveal them later, and the median completion percentage determines payout. Majority jurors can claim rewards.

seven.question: Where can I check reputation?
seven.answer: The reputation page shows average ratings and recovery status for a wallet when the ReputationRegistry is configured.

FAQ.detailBody: The developer docs cover deployment, faucets, tests, and contract behavior.

About.title: Escrow infrastructure for freelance work.
About.intro: TrustLedger combines EVM smart-contract custody, encrypted contract drafting, privacy-safe analytics, juror arbitration, and reputation history into one open-source workflow. This page is intentionally small for now so project background can grow without cluttering the task surfaces.

custody.title: Custody
custody.body: Escrow state and payout rules live in smart contracts. The app explains states and actions without taking custody.

dispute.title: Dispute Resolution
dispute.body: Staked jurors use commit-reveal voting so contested work can resolve without a platform operator deciding the result.

readiness.title: Operational Readiness
readiness.body: CI, docs, audit-readiness reports, and admin observability are treated as product features, not afterthoughts.

timer.title: Project Stopwatch
timer.body: Counting from May 2, 2026, when TrustLedger began.

Legal.title: Policies, disclosures, and compliance references
Legal.intro: This page is the formal publication index for TrustLedger legal documents. Legal content is maintained in Markdown and can be translated with a human-review workflow before publication.

metadata.title: Legal Center - TrustLedger
metadata.description: TrustLedger legal, policy, compliance, risk disclosure, and security document index.

translationHelper.title: Translation Helper
translationHelper.body: Use this helper before publishing legal text in another language. For English, it becomes a review prompt. For other languages, it becomes a translation prompt that keeps numbering, defined terms, links, and Markdown structure intact.
translationHelper.stepOne: 1. Choose the legal document and target locale.
translationHelper.stepTwo: 2. Translate without changing obligations or remedies.
translationHelper.stepThree: 3. Keep the result marked needs-review until approved.

security.title: Security and Reporting
security.body: Security disclosures remain routed through the repository security policy. Legal content changes should be reviewed before publication and mirrored into documentation.

terms.title: Terms and Conditions
terms.description: User obligations, platform scope, wallet activity, escrow actions, and dispute handling.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.description: Personal data handling, wallet identifiers, operational logs, analytics, and user rights.

cookies.title: Cookie Policy
cookies.description: Browser storage, cookies, analytics preferences, and consent expectations.

acceptable-use.title: Acceptable Use Policy
acceptable-use.description: Prohibited activity, sanctions-sensitive conduct, abuse controls, and enforcement paths.

content.title: Content Policy
content.description: Rules for contract links, deliverables, evidence uploads, and platform-visible materials.

dmca.title: DMCA Policy
dmca.description: Copyright takedown notices, counter-notices, repeat infringer handling, and agent details.

trademark.title: Trademark Policy
trademark.description: Brand use, impersonation, confusing marks, and reporting requirements.

risk.title: Risk Disclosure
risk.description: Blockchain, wallet, smart contract, market, arbitration, and availability risks.

disclaimer.title: Disclaimer
disclaimer.description: No legal, financial, tax, or investment advice and no guarantee of dispute outcomes.

community.title: Community Guidelines
community.description: Professional conduct, evidence quality, juror behavior, and communication standards.

security.title: Security Policy
security.description: Responsible disclosure, supported security scope, and vulnerability reporting.

Account.title: Wallet-Gated Profile And Preferences
Account.intro: TrustLedger account services use signed wallet authentication for profile preferences, notifications, and encrypted in-app messaging metadata. The service never asks for passwords, seed phrases, private keys, raw documents, or session keys.
Account.securityNoDocuments: Keep Documents And Keys Out Of Account APIs
Account.profilePreferencesBody: Display name, avatar URL, notification preferences, and dashboard onboarding state can follow the wallet across devices once a signed session exists.
Account.encryptedMessagingBody: The API boundary is designed for encrypted message envelopes; plaintext personal contact details do not belong in platform messages.
Account.externalDatabaseBody: Production persistence should use Postgres, Supabase, or another managed database configured through deployment secrets.
Account.autoLogoutBody: Disconnect the wallet automatically after this period of inactivity.
Account.autoLogoutNote: Takes effect on the next wallet connection.

metadata.title: Platform Status | TrustLedger
metadata.description: Public TrustLedger operations center for runtime health, privacy-safe analytics, and contract summary metrics.

Status.title: Public Operations Center
Status.intro: Review public runtime health, privacy-safe analytics artifacts, and contract summary metrics without exposing admin tokens, RPC credentials, API keys, raw documents, or private wallet material.

github.title: GitHub Project Activity
github.body: Public repository activity for {repository}. This section appears only when the configured GitHub repository is public.

NotFound.title: This route is not in the ledger.
NotFound.body: The link may be old, locale-specific, or tied to a contract that is not available from this route. Use the actions below to get back to a known workflow.
NotFound.rootTitle: This TrustLedger route does not exist.
NotFound.rootBody: Choose a locale-aware route to continue.

Admin.title: TrustLedger Admin Dashboard
Admin.intro: Read-only operational overview for health, contracts, disputes, jurors, reputation, notifications, oracle freshness, deployment metadata, security reports, rate limits, and audit-readiness.
Admin.mutationWarning: Admin mutations are disabled. Future actions must require explicit confirmation, server-side authorization, and persistent audit trails before they are enabled.
Admin.healthBody: Read-only status distribution across server, Vercel, analytics, contracts, notifications, oracle, and security checks.
Admin.trafficBody: Aggregate page views and frontend errors. Private wallet data, IPs, user agents, cookies, and query strings are excluded.
Admin.trafficEmpty: No aggregate traffic events are stored for the current runtime.
Admin.signInIntro: Use a configured admin account. Passwords are verified against server-side PBKDF2 hashes, and access can also be restricted by IP and wallet allowlist.

Stats.title: Codebase Statistics
Stats.intro: A transparent look at the scale of the TrustLedger codebase: total lines of code, files, directories, and the file-type categories that make it up. Every figure is computed from git-tracked source files and contains no private information.
Stats.generatedNote: Statistics regenerate on every production build, so they always reflect the latest source.
Stats.categoriesIntro: Share of source lines by file-type category across the tracked codebase. Only categories present in the repository are shown.
